summary: A high‑energy 1982 Santa Fe Downs show recorded on a solid audience tape, highlighted by scorching Throwing Stones and an unforgettable Other One.
review: The concert is repeatedly described as one of the best shows of ’82 – an epic first set followed by a second set that many fans call “even better,” with standout moments like Throw & Stones, The Other One, Althea and a special Brent‑added verse in Never Trust A Woman.  The audience recording is praised for its lively vibe and overall clarity despite occasional glitches (notably a cut in Althea), making it a very listenable document of a classic Dead night.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews are 5‑star, lauding both the performance and the tape’s sound; only a few unrelated low scores appear.  The show is consistently called exceptional while the recording is described as good with minor technical flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and lively audience mix; reviewers note a crisp feel, but mention occasional tech glitches—a noticeable cut/gap in Althea and some minor drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Never Trust a Woman (special Santa Fe verse)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans rave about an epic first set and a second set that kept the energy soaring, with seamless transitions after the drums and a string of high‑impact numbers that felt like two shows in one.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for strong vocals (“Jerry magic”), scorching guitar tone (smoke boiling off his axe) and a particularly great first verse of Half Step.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as roaring into The Other One, driving the band into a frenzy with powerful bass work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland gets kudos for a special Santa Fe verse in Never Trust A Woman and tasteful slide embellishments throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: The after‑drums segment is highlighted as a highlight, with Bill (and Mickey) providing a dynamic launch into the second‑set marathon.
